data.title: As a developer, T13 and T4 kudos triggers fire for duplicate-block reduction in Python, JS/TS, Ruby, and Java projects
data.description:
## Description

**What**: Replace the Python ast.dump() subtree-hashing implementations of both T13 (Kudos Epic 1) and T4 (Kudos Epic 2) with a jscpd-based approach using the JSON reporter to a temp directory. Emit TRIGGER_SKIPPED when jscpd is absent.

**Why**: T13 and T4 both use Python AST and produce zero awards on Ruby and JS/TS codebases. jscpd supports Python, JS/TS, Ruby, and Java and produces structured JSON output suitable for before/after comparison.

**Scope**:
- IN: `jscpd --reporters=json --output=<tempdir>` subprocess with 30s timeout; parse JSON output for duplicate count; T13 compares before/after within the session diff; T4 compares current vs merge-base duplicate count; both emit TRIGGER_SKIPPED when jscpd absent or subprocess exits non-zero; award kudos on measurable reduction
- OUT: T7, T9, T12; installing jscpd in CI (Story E)

**Note on T13 vs T4 asymmetry**: T13 (Epic 1) measures duplicate reduction within the current session diff (no snapshot dependency). T4 (Epic 2, snapshot-dependent) compares current duplicate count against `.kudos/test-gate-snapshot.json` baseline captured at worktree creation. Both use the same jscpd tool and JSON parsing logic, but T4 requires the snapshot file to exist (skip if absent, do not emit TRIGGER_SKIPPED — the snapshot infrastructure is delivered by Kudos Epic 2 SC-E2-1).

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, T13 fires when jscpd detects a measurable reduction in duplicate blocks within the session diff for a non-Python file (Ruby or JS/TS fixture)
  ← Satisfies: "T13 and T4 use jscpd (JSON reporter to temp dir, 30s timeout, exit code parsing)"
- When this story is complete, T4 fires when jscpd detects a measurable reduction in duplicate blocks vs the merge-base snapshot (`.kudos/test-gate-snapshot.json`)
  ← Satisfies: "T4 duplicate block reduction"
- When this story is complete, both T13 and T4 emit TRIGGER_SKIPPED when jscpd is not on PATH or exits non-zero
  ← Satisfies: "when jscpd absent: TRIGGER_SKIPPED"
- When this story is complete, T4 silently skips (does not emit TRIGGER_SKIPPED) when the snapshot file is absent, because snapshot creation is Kudos Epic 2's responsibility
  ← Satisfies: T4 snapshot dependency contract
- When this story is complete, TRIGGER_SKIPPED signal fields conform to Kudos Epic 2 (71df-5bd8) schema; no shared library imported from Kudos Epic 2
  ← Satisfies: "All five triggers emit machine-readable TRIGGER_SKIPPED signal"
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for: jscpd-absent case, timeout case, T13 reduction case (award), T4 snapshot-present-with-reduction case (award), T4 snapshot-absent case (silent skip)

## Considerations

- [Testing] Mock `subprocess.run` for jscpd; mock filesystem for snapshot file presence/absence; test temp dir creation and cleanup
- [Reliability] Temp dir must be created (tempfile.mkdtemp) and cleaned up (finally block) regardless of outcome
- [Maintainability] TRIGGER_SKIPPED signal format: reference Kudos Epic 2 (71df-5bd8) for the required field schema; implement independently (no import from Kudos Epic 2 modules)
- [Reliability] dispatch_kudos_trigger() from Kudos Epic 1 (3e9b-afee) must be mocked in unit tests
- [Reliability] T4 snapshot dependency: `.kudos/test-gate-snapshot.json` is created by kudos-snapshot.sh (Kudos Epic 2 SC-E2-1); if absent, T4 silently skips (this is expected behavior during testing without Epic 2 deployed)

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
